A Love Letter From Paula
Without ever meeting you, it’s as if we are kindred spirits.
Inside you beats a heart that desires to dance with joy, and celebrate life. You wish to relish the
time you have on this precious earth inside a body that affirms your highest ambitions; your
greatest dreams. You long to be heard, and ache to be truly seen.
During the quietest of moments, there speaks a familiar voice—the one you listened to as a child
with glee and optimism. Call it your conscious, your inner child, or the voice of your soul. She
speaks to you from afar, and is asking you to dream big, once again. She asks you to love and
believe in yourself without conditions, to embrace your fears as if they were the warmest of
allies, and jump into life head first, and head strong. She pleads with you to hold your body and
your health as close to your heart as you do your family and friends, and treat it with the same
respect and care as you would your child, your mate, or your lover.
She is challenging you to live out loud.
I, for one, believe you are ready, otherwise you would not be reading this letter. And because you
are here with me, today, I know we are very much alike. That part of you that has decided to
listen to your inner voice again, with a bit more faith, with a touch more self love, and with
strength you know you have within, is the part of you I love. It’s the same part I love within
myself as well.
Like you, I too have had many moments where I failed to listen to that inner voice; where I chose
instead to punish my body for crimes it never committed. The stress of work, the pressure of
expectations, the need to relax and find relief, the desire to be truly loved and accepted; these are
all the hallmark of a fellow journeyer in this life to me, and a sister long lost and finally found.
They are also traits common to those of us whose bodies and hearts became whipping posts, and
the target of all our frustrations.
We have paid the price, haven’t we? Perhaps like me, your price is lingering physical pain, or
maybe you see a false reflection in the mirror; one that the world recognizes as normal, yet you
see as flawed. Within these pages, you’ll discover a side of me that the world and the media that
controls it has rarely understood. I plan to reveal much of my own soul to you in order to gain
your trust, and hopefully inspire your revival.
There are many other sisters out there who chose a different method of payment. For example,
the weight you may have gained over the years to insulate yourself from the emotional, physical,
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 1 of 2 --

or spiritual torture may be your form of personal expression—your psychological method of
dealing with it all.
Whatever the price you’ve paid, and however you opted to pay it, you will find no judgment
here. Here, within these pages, and from the depths of my heart, you will find a fellow traveler.
Consider me a companion, a friend, and a shoulder to cry upon, even though we are separated
temporarily by miles or by oceans.
If you read this letter with your eyes and your soul, you will hear its message to you. You will
heed my plea to reach deep inside, rediscover that precious inner voice of strength and
innocence, and set about on a journey to reclaim your body, your spirit, and your life.
This journey begins by embracing the only ‘you’ you will ever have. So, I implore you to choose
today to love, accept, and forgive your self before we even take the first step together. You may
believe right now that the most challenging step for you will be the first step inside a gym, or
perhaps down the vegetable isle in the grocery store, or onto the yoga floor in soon-to-be
flattering tights. As difficult as those steps may be for you, it is the step you must take toward
your self that is the most critical, and perhaps the one that requires the most courage.
If you are still reading this letter, you have that courage. I know you do.
Throughout this book, in each and every chapter, I will place mini love letters within the pages to
remind you of this all-important step: to fully love, accept, and forgive your self on a daily basis.
Without this, there can be no lasting physical change. You will simply be reliving the shame from
within a leaner body, and struggling against the non-acceptance while wearing a smaller dress
size.
Believe me when I tell you—this step toward loving your self is the most important step you will
ever take in your life. Take this step with me, and you will eventually end up dancing your way
through life.
As you see within these pages, I am not going to go light on the how-to sections. As someone
known throughout the world for her work in choreography, I know the value of following in
someone’s footsteps. This book is filled with my life choreographers—men and women who I
discovered along my own journey that I plan to introduce to you. Their methods of healing,
nutrition planning, exercise, and thought inspired the blueprint for whole body health you now
hold in your hands. I am not an expert in any of these fields; rather, I am a lifelong student, and a
voice for my fellow sisters. I want everyone to hear the hope we now have, as modern women, to
reclaim our bodies and our health.
So, let’s begin right now with your first dancing lesson, shall we?
— Paula Abdul
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com
